data.title: US Senate Bill Limits Stablecoin Yields; Franklin Templeton Upgrade Fund
data.description:
Headline

▌US Senate Crypto Market Structure Bill Proposes Restrictions on Stablecoin Yields, May Face Stricter Amendments

US Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott released the 278-page text of the Crypto Market Structure Bill, proposing to divide regulatory power over digital assets between the SEC and CFTC, and clarify the securities and commodities attributes. The issue of stablecoin yields has become a core point of contention. The draft prohibits digital asset service providers from paying any interest or yields to users who only hold payment-type stablecoins, but allows for exceptions based on incentives such as trading, staking, liquidity provision, or collateral. Sources say that stricter amendments, which may significantly restrict stablecoin yields, may be submitted and could be passed. Meanwhile, ethical clauses concerning Trump and his family's crypto businesses remain unresolved, which Democrats consider a legislative "red line." The bill will enter the revision and voting stage in the committee this week.

Franklin Templeton Upgrades Two Money Market Funds to Facilitate Blockchain and Stablecoin Applications Franklin Templeton announced updates to two of its institutional-grade money market funds to make them available for the emerging tokenized finance and regulated stablecoin markets. The changes involve funds managed by its affiliate, Western Asset Management, and aim to help institutional clients use familiar cash management tools within blockchain platforms and stablecoin reserve frameworks. The Western Asset Institutional Treasury Obligations Fund has adjusted its investment structure to comply with the stablecoin reserve asset requirements of the U.S. GENIUS Act, currently holding only U.S. Treasury securities with maturities of no more than 93 days, which can be used as reserve assets for compliant stablecoins. The other fund, the Western Asset Institutional Treasury Reserves Fund, has launched a new digital institutional share class, allowing approved intermediaries to record and transfer fund share ownership through blockchain infrastructure. Franklin Templeton stated that this move aims to help institutional investors gradually adopt on-chain infrastructure without introducing entirely new crypto-native products. This development also echoes recent moves by large financial institutions such as JPMorgan Chase to launch tokenized money market products on Ethereum. As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $95,339.80, a 24-hour change of +4.6%; ETH price is $3,325.90, a 24-hour change of +7.7%; BNB price is $943.32, a 24-hour change of +4.2%. SOL price is $146.12, up 5.0% in the last 24 hours; DOGE price is $0.1486, up 8.7% in the last 24 hours; XRP price is $2.16, up 5.4% in the last 24 hours; TRX price is $0.3064, up 2.3% in the last 24 hours. WLFI price is $0.1805, up 6.8% in the last 24 hours; HYPE price is $25.57, up 7.8% in the last 24 hours. Policy The committee announced on Tuesday that the hearing, originally scheduled for January 15 and postponed to Monday, would be held at 3 p.m. that day. This hearing is a crucial step in advancing the legislation, allowing lawmakers to debate amendments, vote on whether to include them in the full bill, and then vote on whether to submit the complete bill to the full Senate for consideration. The Senate Banking Committee will hold a hearing on its version of the bill this Thursday. The draft bill from the Banking Committee was released around midnight Monday, but lawmakers are expected to submit amendments before the hearing. Senator Elizabeth Warren: Trump-owned WLFI bank application should be suspended. Senator Elizabeth Warren has called for a suspension of bank applications related to World Liberty Financial (WLFI) until President Trump divests his family's stake in the company and related digital asset businesses. Warren, a senior Democrat on the Senate Banking Committee, made this request before the committee's planned consideration of the cryptocurrency market structure bill, citing significant ethical concerns regarding the application. In a letter to Jonathan Gould, the Director General of the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, Warren stated that approving the bank's application could allow the president to directly influence his own company's profits and regulate its operations, raising ethical concerns for the government. The UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) has expressed concern about the sale of complex ETPs to retail investors, warning of gaps in risk disclosure and customer due diligence that could undermine the protections afforded under the Consumer Duty. In a review report released on January 12, the FCA examined how companies of different sizes and business models market and distribute complex ETPs to individual investors. The report revealed inconsistencies: some companies practiced well, clearly targeting their markets, rigorously assessing customer knowledge, and continuously monitoring purchases; however, others relied on superficial or weak checks and disclosures, failing to effectively help retail investors understand the associated risks. The FCA stated that these weaknesses could expose investors to product risks they do not fully understand. Complex ETPs represent a small percentage of the overall market but are high-risk. These include leveraged and inverse strategies, as well as exchange-traded notes (ETNs) linked to cryptocurrencies. These product structures can amplify returns, but can also accelerate losses if held for too long or in volatile markets.

Blockchain Applications

▌Sources: CoinGecko Considering Sale at Approximately $500 Million Valuation

According to sources, cryptocurrency data platform CoinGecko is considering a sale and has hired investment bank Moelis to handle the process. The cryptocurrency market data company is valued at approximately $500 million. This potential sale comes at a time of rapidly accelerating cryptocurrency M&A activity, with disclosed M&A deals reaching approximately $8.6 billion in 2025, totaling a record 133 transactions.

▌SSPAC Affiliated with Kraken Files for $250 Million IPO

KrakAcquisition, a new blank-check company backed by Kraken, Tribe Capital, and Natural Capital, filed documents with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Monday, planning to raise up to $250 million through an initial public offering (IPO). According to the SEC filing, KrakAcquisition, incorporated in the Cayman Islands in July 2025, is an exempt company and plans to issue 25 million units at $10 each, and is expected to apply for listing on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker symbol “KRAQU”. While the SPAC targets companies in the crypto infrastructure sector, Kraken reportedly filed a separate confidential Form S-1 in November seeking to list its common stock in a potential IPO.

During the maintenance, all trading and transfer functions will be suspended. Users should complete their operations in advance and check their order status. Trading will gradually resume through price discovery auctions. Cryptocurrency

JPMorgan Chase CFO Warns of Stablecoin Interest-Paying Risks: A “Parallel Banking System Without Regulation”

JPMorgan Chase Chief Financial Officer Jeremy Barnum stated during the Q4 earnings call that the practice of offering stablecoin yields to users in the crypto industry is “clearly dangerous and undesirable,” arguing that this is equivalent to building a “parallel banking system” with features of banks, such as interest-paying deposits, in the absence of prudent regulation. He pointed out that such products could bring systemic risks similar to bank deposits, but are not bound by centuries of banking regulatory frameworks. Barnum’s remarks come as the U.S. Senate Banking Committee released a new draft bill on the structure of the crypto market, which proposes to restrict stablecoin issuers and crypto platforms from directly offering yields to users unless linked to specific activities such as staking. Barnum emphasized that JPMorgan Chase is willing to compete with crypto products, but only if the relevant services operate within a suitable regulatory framework.

▌Grayscale Updates Q1 2026 "Assets Under Consideration" List, Adding 36 Altcoins

Grayscale has updated a list of assets under consideration for future investment products, as well as a new list of existing assets. The "Assets Under Consideration" list includes digital assets not currently included in Grayscale's investment products, but which the team has identified as potential candidates for future inclusion. This list adds 36 altcoins, with candidate companies spanning five major sectors: smart contracts, finance, consumer and culture, artificial intelligence, and utilities and services. Smart contract platforms and finance have the most assets listed. However, inclusion on this list does not guarantee that an asset will be included in an investment portfolio; it only indicates that Grayscale is actively evaluating these assets.

Vitalik Again Exchanges Some Free Tokens for 9.4 ETH According to Lookonchain, Ethereum founder Vitalik sold some of his free tokens from his wallet in the past 30 minutes, receiving 9.4 ETH (approximately $29,400). Strive will hold nearly 12,800 bitcoins after acquiring Semler. Strive shareholders have approved the acquisition of Semler Scientific, but investors were surprised by the 1-for-20 reverse stock split accompanying the deal, causing Strive's stock price to fall by 12% and Semler's stock price to fall by nearly 10%. Following the merger, Strive will hold approximately 12,798 bitcoins, exceeding the holdings of Tesla and Trump Media & Technology Group. Strive plans to profit from Semler's medical diagnostics business and repay approximately $120 million in debt, while maintaining a streamlined corporate structure and focusing on its Bitcoin business and revenue generation.

▌XRP, SOL, and 6 other tokens will enjoy the same treatment as BTC and ETH from the date the Clarity Act takes effect

According to crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett, the draft Clarity Act contains a noteworthy clause based on whether certain tokens have been included in ETFs by January 1, 2026, thus classifying them as non-ancillary assets. The clause stipulates that if a token is already a core asset of an ETF by January 1, 2026, and that ETF is listed on a national stock exchange and registered under Section 6 of the Securities Exchange Act, then that token will not be subject to the information disclosure obligations required of other tokens. In other words, under this framework, XRP, SOL, LTC, HBAR, DOGE, and LINK will be treated the same as BTC and ETH from the date the act takes effect.

▌Coinbase to Launch Raydium (RAY) Spot Trading

Coinbase will launch Raydium (RAY) spot trading. The RAY-USD trading pair will begin trading if liquidity conditions are met and the supported trading regions meet the requirements.

Important Economic Developments

▌US December CPI Rises 2.7% Year-on-Year, Meeting Market Expectations

The US Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 2.7% year-on-year in December, in line with market expectations and unchanged from the previous month.

▌Following CPI Release, the Probability of a Fed Rate Cut in April Rises to 42%

On Tuesday, traders increased their bets, believing the Fed might not wait until Chairman Powell's term ends in May before cutting rates, after the Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that basic consumer prices rose slightly less than expected. Although traders still believe a June rate cut is the most likely outcome, the latest data predicts a 42% probability of a Fed rate cut in April, up from 38% before the data release.

▌Fed Mouthpiece: December CPI Unlikely to Change Fed's Current Wait-and-See Stance

Fed mouthpiece Nick Timiraos said the December Consumer Price Index (CPI) is unlikely to change the Fed's current wait-and-see stance, as officials are likely to want to see more evidence that inflation is stabilizing and gradually declining before cutting rates.

The Federal Reserve has cut its benchmark interest rate in three consecutive meetings, most recently in December, even though inflation stopped falling last year. Officials lowered rates due to concerns about a potentially larger-than-expected slowdown in the labor market. For rate cuts to resume, Fed officials may need to see new evidence that labor market conditions are deteriorating or that price pressures are easing. The latter may require at least several more months of inflation data to become apparent.

▌Trump: Fed Chair Nominee to be Determined in Coming Weeks

President Trump stated that the Fed Chair nominee will be determined in the coming weeks.

▌Fed Chair Candidate Rieder Speaks Out: Supports Rate Cut to 3%

BlackRock's Global Chief Fixed Income Investment Officer, Rick Rieder, a potential successor to Fed Chair Powell, is reportedly scheduled to meet with President Trump on Thursday.

At this juncture, Riddell reiterated his support for lowering the U.S. benchmark interest rate to 3%, a level that would be the lowest in more than three years. In a CNBC interview aired on Monday, Riddell stated that he had repeatedly expressed his desire for rates to fall to 3% for months. He reiterated his support for this move on Monday, which would reduce borrowing costs by at least 50 basis points (0.5 percentage points) from current levels. The current target range for the federal funds rate is 3.5%–3.75%, following a 25-basis-point rate cut by Federal Reserve officials in December. “I think the Fed does have some policy room,” Riddell said. “My position has been very clear over the past few months. The Fed must lower rates, and I don’t think it needs to be lowered too much, eventually to 3%—that level is closer to the neutral rate.” The neutral rate is a theoretical level of borrowing costs that is neither stimulative nor restrictive, and can maintain the smooth operation of the U.S. economy.

▌The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in January is 97.2%

According to CME's "FedWatch": The probability of the Fed cutting interest rates by 25 basis points in January is 2.8%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 97.2%. By March, the cumulative probability of a 25 basis point rate cut is 26.8%, the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 72.5%, and the cumulative probability of a 50 basis point rate cut is 0.7%.

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Why is DAT Treasury stock falling faster than its asset holdings?

DAT Treasury's reserve strategy has been effective during market rallies. Stocks of companies holding large amounts of cryptocurrency assets tend to outperform their asset holdings, attracting investors who want to invest in cryptocurrencies but do not want to hold them directly.

However, this relationship collapses dramatically during market downturns. Crypto treasury stocks tend to fall more sharply than the cryptocurrencies they hold. This pattern is not accidental but stems from the interplay between the stock market, corporate balance sheets, and investor behavior. Theoretically, if a company holds a significant amount of cryptocurrency, its market capitalization should move in tandem with the asset's price. But in reality, history has shown otherwise. During market sell-offs, these stocks often underperform the cryptocurrencies they hold. This is because buying these stocks is not the same as buying Bitcoin. Investors are buying equity in a highly leveraged, market-sentiment-driven company that holds Bitcoin. This distinction becomes crucial when market risk appetite diminishes. In an uptrend, crypto treasury stocks typically trade above their net asset value. When market sentiment shifts, these expectations vanish. Many investors begin to prioritize downside protection and balance sheet robustness over growth potential. The premium quickly narrows, often turning into a discount. Therefore, the stock price decline is not only due to the decrease in the value of cryptocurrencies, but also to the shrinking valuation multiples. During periods of heightened risk aversion, funds are more likely to shift from alternative assets such as stocks to ETFs, or even withdraw entirely from the cryptocurrency market, thus accelerating premium compression. This structural shift has resulted in a faster and greater rate of premium compression than in previous cycles.

data.title: A renowned independent crypto analyst released his top ten predictions for 2026.
data.description:
Author: Miles Deutscher & Translation: Jia Huan, ChainCatcher

In this article, I will break down my top 10 cryptocurrency predictions for 2026 in detail. This includes my $BTC price prediction, top altcoin narratives, the prospects of combining crypto with AI, and more. This is an exercise I do every year, and it really helps me calibrate my direction for the new year. Even if you disagree with all the predictions here, I hope it will inspire you to make your own list to prepare for the year ahead and capture the biggest upside potential.

10. Prediction Market Trading Volume to Grow 5x

From January 2025 to today, prediction market trading volume has grown approximately 10x. I believe this trend will continue, and in 2026, we will see at least one month with trading volume reaching 5x current levels.

A fivefold increase in current trading volume would equate to approximately $95 billion per month. Another factor to consider is the rise of “neighbor protocols” built on top of prediction markets. For example, @intodotspace is building the first leveraged prediction market. This trend will further amplify trading volume.

9. Stock/Metal Perpetual Contract Trading Boom

The argument here is simple: we will continue to see perpetual contracts spread across the mainstream—not just limited to crypto token trading.

... 2025 will be a breakout year for perpetual futures technology on crypto tracks—instant settlement, excellent UI/UX, decentralization, and more. Secondly, gold/metals/stocks are in a strong bull market. Typically, as cycles near their peak, people tend to move further down the risk curve in pursuit of returns (perpetual contracts are well-suited to benefit alongside small-cap/emerging markets). In many cases, accessing crypto tracks is easier and faster than buying stocks/gold through traditional financial (TradFi) tracks. Due to this combined effect, I believe we will see an explosive growth in stock/metal trading volume on perpetual DEXs, and perpetual contract trading will extend far beyond crypto token trading volume. 8. The ICO "Renaissance" Continues Due to the dramatic shift in the regulatory environment, the public now has unprecedented opportunities to participate in token sales. ICO participation is at an all-time high, and I expect this overall upward trend to continue. If you're interested in profiting from ICOs, I recently released a complete Tweet Guide and a free Notion template to help you stay organized in this space—you can check it out below.

7. Altcoins with Strong Revenue Models Will Win

The market trend has clearly shifted, favoring genuine businesses over mere speculation and hype. Of course, the latter will always have a place in the market, but more and more investors (large and small) are looking for a real "flywheel" to support token prices (in a sea of ​​token dilution).

I believe protocols that actually generate revenue will continue to dominate. Simply put, protocols with real revenue mechanisms will thrive better than those without. Pay attention to companies/projects/teams that are actually generating revenue.

6. RWA is poised for its second-best year ever. I'd love to say RWA is going to have its biggest year ever. However, technically, it will be difficult to surpass the $14 billion growth projected for 2025. I believe liquidity will continue to flow into the space, making it another bumper year, but the percentage growth will likely be lower than in 2025. 2026 is likely to bring many yield/tokenization opportunities and a significant presence in altcoin trading. 5. Digital Banks (Neobanks) Become the Most Exponentially Growing Sector in Crypto Space It feels like we've finally reached a turning point for crypto/stablecoin banking. The infrastructure has finally caught up with demand, and we're seeing explosive growth in the adoption of many products. The ability to easily exchange cryptocurrencies for fiat currencies is a much-needed solution. This sector has the largest potential market (basically the entire financial world) and is actively addressing issues in developing countries and regions where traditional financial systems are less convenient. This is a huge market, and I expect we will finally (truly) conquer it this year. 4. AI / AI Agents Are Truly Back Last January, we either experienced a crazy AI hype season that drove up the price of Crypto x AI protocols—the only problem was that the technology hadn't caught up yet. Entering 2026, things are different; the technology can actually deliver on the hype's promise. To me, 2026 will undoubtedly be the most pivotal year for AI to date (like every year), and as people speculate on the upside potential of AI, retail interest can easily spill over into the crypto space. Crypto x AI is the perfect synergy. Crypto brings freedom to the financial track, while AI brings automation, which I believe is the future of finance. I think this will create opportunities in many AI sub-sectors, including x402, robotics, agentic workflows, AI data/infrastructure, and more. 3. Stablecoin Supply Increases by Over 50% Last year, the total supply of stablecoins increased by 50% (from $200 billion to $300 billion). I believe we will see a similar increase in stablecoin supply this year, further catalyzed by the Genius Act in the United States, which regulates stablecoins.

2. Institutional Drivers Will Outpace Retail Investors

This entire cycle has been driven by institutions (DATs, ETFs, etc.). I expect this shift towards an institutionally driven market to continue—which is why I'm focusing on tokens/protocols that can attract institutional interest (returning to my point about focusing on projects with real revenue).

1. BTC Price Will Be Higher at Year-End

Whether $BTC will experience a crazy "peak and fall" above $150,000 this year depends on many factors.

Funding flows/buyer demand, DATs, macroeconomic background, etc. Frankly, I'm not sure if the market can align with all the right factors to trigger the most extreme bullish topping pattern. However, I believe Bitcoin will close with a bullish price candle at the end of 2026. This means $BTC must close above the $90,000 range. To summarize my bullish rationale (I may post a more detailed post on this later): We are likely in the final year of a business cycle, and we are seeing similar price bottoming behavior to previous years, which makes me believe we will at least close in the green this year—I will delve deeper into my exact argument in the coming weeks, so stay tuned.
